task_description: # Extract HTML Content in n8n ## Background You have an n8n workflow that fetches a webpage. The HTML content is currently stored in the `data` field of the JSON output. You need to add an HTML Extract node to extract specific elements from the HTML. ## Requirements Modify the `/home/user/workflow.json` file to include an `HtmlExtract` node (type `n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract`) connected after the `Mock HTML` node. Configure the HTML Extract node to extract the following information from the HTML in the `data` property: 1. The title of the page (`` tag), save it as `page_title` (return value: `text`). 2. The main heading (`<h1 class="heading">`), save it as `main_heading` (return value: `text`). 3. The URL of the first image (`<img>` tag), save it as `image_url` by extracting the `src` attribute (return value: `attribute`). ## Implementation Guide 1. Open `/home/user/workflow.json`. 2. Add a new node of type `n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract` named `HTML Extract`. 3. Set its `dataPropertyName` to `data`. 4. Add the three required extraction values in the `extractionValues.values` parameter. 5. Update the `connections` object so that the `Mock HTML` node connects to the `HTML Extract` node. ## Constraints - Project path: /home/user - Do not remove the existing nodes. - Ensure the JSON syntax is valid.
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. `cd /home/user`

## Verification Steps
1. Parse `workflow.json`.
2. Verify that a node named `HTML Extract` exists and has the correct type `n8n-nodes-base.htmlExtract`.
3. Verify that the node is connected from `Mock HTML`.
4. Verify that the node extracts `page_title` using the `title` CSS selector with `text` return value.
5. Verify that the node extracts `main_heading` using the `h1.heading` CSS selector with `text` return value.
6. Verify that the node extracts `image_url` using the `img` CSS selector with `attribute` return value and `src` attribute.

## Expected Results
- The `workflow.json` contains the configured HTML Extract node and it is properly connected.
